trim_reasons: VOLATILE tag on an AST T4 pick with no iron_floor means the distribution is wide — a single quiet playmaking night easily misses. The 70% confidence reflects the structural softness, but AST props for volatile players at T4+ have confirmed suppression risk per historical audit.

VOLATILE tag combined with FG_COLD:-9% and trend=down on a secondary scorer is the exact profile the audit history flagged as structurally fragile — efficiency-dependent scoring with a cold-shooting signal means one off night from the field lands this well below 10. Historical lessons specifically note FG_COLD on secondary-scorer PTS should carry meaningful weight even at T10.

Audit history documents a confirmed multi-instance Bane blowout PTS miss pattern as a secondary scorer, and the system now flags Bane PTS at spread ≥10 when ORL is favored; this spread is only 2.5 but the min_floor of 17.9 (vs avg 29.3) exposes extreme minutes fragility — a single low-usage game produces a miss. The post_miss_hr of 67% (lift=0.87) further confirms below-average recovery probability on this prop.

Min_floor of 17.9 vs avg 29.3 is the most extreme minutes fragility on the slate — Bane's AST accumulation is entirely contingent on logging meaningful minutes, and any game where he plays his floor (~18 min) renders the T2 AST threshold very difficult to clear. Iron_floor protects the hit rate in normal games but does not insulate against a genuine low-minutes outcome.

VOLATILE tag with a min_floor of only 24.8 (avg 30.8) on a T20 PTS pick means a single low-minutes game — well within Kawhi's documented load-management variance — produces a miss. LAC is favored by 5.5, adding blowout/Q4-rest risk for a player already known for managed minutes, and the VOLATILE + FG_MARGIN_NEG flags compound the fragility.

FG_COLD:-17% is well above the audit-documented threshold where cold-shooting becomes structurally predictive for secondary-scorer PTS misses, combined with VOLATILE, FG_MARGIN_THIN:10%, and a min_floor of only 23.4 — one below-average shooting night (already the recent trend) on a player with thin minutes protection produces a miss. This is the exact FG_COLD + volatile + secondary-scorer combination the historical lessons identified as warranting at minimum a soft skip.

VOLATILE on an AST T6 pick with no iron_floor, trend=down, and a min_floor of 23.4 creates layered fragility: AST accumulation at T6+ requires both significant minutes and active playmaking usage, neither of which is guaranteed. The audit history confirms VOLATILE + AST T6+ is a hard gate tier, and 70% confidence reflects genuine structural softness.

Team momentum=cold combined with VOLATILE, a post_miss_hr of only 67% (lift=0.93), and a min_floor of 25.0 on a player averaging just 27.5 minutes means a cold night from a team already in a slump could compress both usage and output. The typical_miss of 5.5 pts below threshold signals when he misses, he misses badly — the floor is not sticky.
